summarization.a: Schematic of the initial clean Ni metal surface prior to plasma exposure.
summarization.b: Schematic illustrating plasma ion bombardment of the Ni surface, initiating surface activation.
summarization.c: Schematic showing saturation of the activated surface layer following continued plasma exposure.
summarization.d: Schematic depicting introduction of etchant molecules after the plasma has been switched off.
summarization.e: Schematic of the dissociative adsorption of etchant molecules on the activated surface, forming volatile metal complexes and water.
summarization.f: Schematic showing termination of the reaction after consumption of modifier atoms and re-exposure of the Ni metal surface.

d.question: At what stage are the etchant molecules introduced relative to the plasma step?
d.answer: After the plasma has been switched off.

f.question: What is the state of the surface after the completion of the etching cycle shown in this panel?
f.answer: The underlying Ni metal surface is exposed again, similar to its initial state.

e.question: How does the formation of volatile products in this step enable the etching process?
e.answer: The reaction between the adsorbed etchant molecules and the activated surface metal atoms forms complex molecules with high vapor pressure. These volatile complexes can easily desorb from the surface into the gas phase. This desorption effectively removes the top layer of metal atoms, resulting in the controlled etching of the material.

b.question: What specific physical process is applied to the metal surface to initiate activation in this step?
